titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Arthur Sze Papers
titlestmt.author: Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday February 26, 2021 at 04:55

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 101-103 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 104-105 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1977-2016. II. Writings, 1972-2016. III. Personal Papers, 1950-2016. IV. Photographs, 1978-2015. V. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1989-2015.

bioghist.head: Arthur Sze (1950-)
bioghist.p: Arthur Sze is an American poet, translator, editor and professor. He was born in New York City in 1950 to Chinese immigrants. He graduated from the University of California, Berkeley in 1972 with a B.A. in poetry, where he also studied classical Chinese. He is the author of ten books of poetry, including his debut The Willow Wind (1972), The Gingko Light (2009), and Compass Rose (2014), which was a finalist for the 2015 Pulitzer Prize. His most recent work, Sight Lines (2019), was awarded the National Book Award. Sze's poetry has also been featured in numerous anthologies, including Premonitions: The Kaya Anthology of New Asian North American Poetry (1995), Verse & Universe: Poems about Science and Mathematics (1998), and New Poets of the American West (2010).

While Sze is best known as an English-language poet, he is also an accomplished Chinese translator. He translated a book of Chinese poetry, The Silk Dragon: Translations from the Chinese (2001) and published a bilingual Chinese-English book in Beijing, Pig's Heaven Inn (2014). He also served as an editor of Chinese Writers on Writing (2010), a collection of works by contemporary Chinese writers and poets.

Sze's poetry is influenced by his Chinese ancestry and his study of classical Chinese language, as well as his experiences living in New Mexico. His work draws from diverse sources of inspiration such as the landscape of the American Southwest, Chinese philosophy, and scientific imagery. His awards include two National Endowment for the Arts Creative Writing Fellowships in 1982 and 1993, the American Book Award in 1996, the Guggenheim Fellowship in 1997, and the Jackson Poetry Prize in 2013. He was recognized as the first poet laureate of Santa Fe in 2006, a title he held until 2008, and served as Chancellor of the American Academy of Poets from 2012 to 2017.

Sze has lived in Santa Fe, New Mexico, for over forty-five years. In addition to his poetry career, he is Professor Emeritus at the Institute of American Indian Arts, where he taught for twenty-two years.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Arthur Sze Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, personal papers, photographs, audio and video recordings, digital files, and other papers related to the life and career of poet Arthur Sze.

Materials including notes, drafts, and manuscripts document Sze's work as a poet, writer, editor, and translator, and provide insight into his creative process. Extensive correspondence reveals his professional relationships with other writers in the United States and abroad, particularly his ties to the American Southwest poetry community and his work with Chinese writers and translators. Correspondents include Jay Wright, Toi Derricotte, Claudia Rankine, Quincy Troupe, Joy Harjo, Leslie Marmon Silko, Simon J. Ortiz, Xi Chuan, Michael Wiegers, Mei-mei Berssenbrugge, Barbara Guest, James Schevill, Layli Long Soldier, Sherwin Bitsui, and Allison Hedge Coke. The collection spans the years 1950-2016, with the bulk of the material dating from 1972-2016.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Arthur Sze Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of letters and printed email exchanges with poets, writers, translators, editors, publishers, literary organizations, creative writing students, family, friends, and others. Among Sze's correspondents are notable Black literary figures such as Jay Wright, Toi Derricotte, Claudia Rankine, and Quincy Troupe, Indigenous poets Joy Harjo (Muscogee Creek), Leslie Marmon Silko (Laguna Pueblo), and Simon J. Ortiz (Acoma Pueblo), and Chinese poet Xi Chuan, as well as editor Michael Wiegers, and poets Mei-mei Berssenbrugge, Barbara Guest, and James Schevill. There is additional correspondence with Sze's former students and colleagues at the Institute of American Indian Art, including Oglala Lakota poet Layli Long Soldier, Navajo poet Sherwin Bitsui, and Allison Hedge Coke, a writer of Huron and Cherokee descent. Writers with whom Sze corresponded frequently sought his feedback, and the series contains a considerable amount of their works, ranging from individual poems to book drafts. Also included are publishing agreements, rights and reproductions permissions, and photographs of Sze.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by last name of correspondent.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Writings.

did.unittitle: Dobkin de Rios, Marlene (see de Rios, Marlene D.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consists of notes, manuscripts, typescripts, proofs, printed works, research materials, reviews, articles and interviews, clippings, correspondence, and related materials pertaining to Sze's published and unpublished poetry, translations, essays, other works, and editing projects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight subseries: Notebooks and wordbooks, Poetry, Books, Chinese Writers on Writing , Shorter works, Printed material, Broadsides, and Writings of others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From 2013 to 2014, Sze reviewed all of his previous poems and recorded his notes, observations, and assessments in a series of twelve bound notebooks found in this subseries. Other notebooks feature preliminary notes for poems, as well as several Chinese-language poems that Sze partially translated or considered translating. Sze's wordbooks are handwritten collections of words and phrases that inspired his work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries is a comprehensive collection of Sze's poetry over the course of his career, including manuscripts of nearly all of his published and unpublished poems from 1972 to 2015. It contains preliminary notes, outlines, drafts at various stages of writing, and final manuscripts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Collected poems, and Individual poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six bound notebooks that encompass Sze's poetry from 1972 to 2015. They contain late-stage and final typescript drafts of poems grouped roughly by their associated poetry collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of files for each poem written between 2000 and 2015, documenting their creation through handwritten notes, typescript drafts, and printouts with annotations and corrections. Sze published many of these poems in literary journals and anthologies before collecting them in his volumes of poetry.

did.unittitle: "The Angle of Reflection Equals the Angle of Incidence"
"Looking Back at the Muckleshoot Reservation on Galisteo Street, Santa Fe"
"Returning to Northern New Mexico after a Trip to Asia"
"Gazing at a Red-Leafed Maple Across a Ravine"
"Mushroom Hunting in the Santa Fe Ski Basin"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials pertaining to Sze's ten published books of poetry, as well as his translated collection of Chinese poetry, The Silk Dragon . The subseries contains manuscripts of his early books, including the original camera-ready copy of his debut, The Willow Wind (1972), as well as typescript drafts with annotations and corrections, and final drafts for publication. There are also reviews of Sze's books.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Manuscripts, and Reviews. Arranged chronologically by publication date of the book.

did.unittitle: Two Ravens , corrected for second edition
"Final, National Poetry Series 1-4, corrected copy 1 and 2," drafts, typescript printouts
"Final" draft, typescript printouts, with list of corrections

title.part: The Silk Dragon: Translations from the Chinese

did.unittitle: The Willow Wind , Two Ravens , Dazzled

title.part: The Silk Dragon: Translations from the Chinese

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chinese Writers on Writing is an anthology of translated Chinese-language essays edited by Sze. This subseries includes correspondence with writers, essay translators and other collaborators on the project, as well as publishing agreements, drafts, annotated and corrected typescripts, and galley proofs.

did.unittitle: Draft, typescript printouts, and background materials
Draft, typescript printouts, and web search

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains essays, notes, and translations by Sze, as well as miscellaneous collected research material. Included are papers related to a translation project supported by a Witter Bynner Foundation for Poetry grant, Sze's work as co-editor of the literary journal Manoa , and "The Silk Road," a poetry and music collaboration with composer Tan Dun that premiered in Santa Fe, New Mexico on April 1, 1989. This subseries also contains early and later drafts of "The Silk Road: Essays and Interviews," Sze's unpublished collection of essays and interviews; other working titles for this project were "The Polysemous Weave" and "The Zigzag Way: Eight Interviews."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title, with research notes filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: "Chinese Poetry in Translation" sponsored by the Witter Bynner Foundation for Poetry
Chinese translations from Yellow Mountain Festival
"Deep Step Come Shine" [on C. D. Wright]
"On Poetry and Water" for the Poetry Foundation
Collected materials for book and preliminary draft, typescript printouts
" The Wang River Sequence , A Prospectus," with drafts, typescript printouts, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains limited edition printings of Sze's poetry, as well as several small anthologies edited by Sze based on his creative writing workshops in New Mexico.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: Cuentos: Telling Our Stories Volume III by elders of nursing and senior centers of Northern New Mexico
Cuentos: Telling Our Stories Volume IV by elders and disabled writers of nursing, senior centers, and adult day programs of Northern New Mexico
Earthshine [limited edition hand-sewn, letterpress chapbook by Felix Press]

title.part: Footprints on Windows: Poems from Santa Fe Public Schools, 1973–1974
I am Waiting to be Free: An Anthology of Women's Writing from the Penitentiary of New Mexico
Retinal Exchange: Poems from the Santa Fe Public Library Workshops and the Institute of American Indian Arts

did.unittitle: A Slanting Light [limited edition artist book by Ellen Sheffield]

title.part: Signs and Portents: Poems from the Santa Fe Public Library Workshops and the Institute of American Indian Arts
Spectral Line: Poems from the Santa Fe Public Library Workshops and the Institute of American Indian Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains prints of Sze's poems and Chinese poetry translations of various sizes, including two subway display posters.

did.unittitle: "From a Painting of a Cat" by Pa-ta-shan-jen, translated by Arthur Sze, broadside
"The Lightning Field #12" by Carol Moldaw and "Thermodynamics" by Arthur Sze [keepsake for reading at Colorado College]
"Do Not Speak Keresan to a Mescalero Apache" for Poetry in Public Places
"Li Po" for Vehicle: Art & Transportation in New Mexico
"Drinking Alone with the Moon" by Li Po, translated by Arthur Sze
"Drinking Wine" by T'ao Ch'ien, translated by Arthur Sze
"A Renga by the Chancellors of the Academy of American Poets"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains articles, essays and interviews about Sze and his writings.

did.unittitle: "Arthur Sze's Taoist Poetic" by Robert Grotjohn
"Arthur Sze's Tessellated Poems" by Gerald Maa

European Journal of American Studies , "On Common Ground: Translocal Attachments and Transethnic Affiliations in Agha Shahid Ali's and Arthur Sze's Poetry of the American Southwest" by Judith Rauscher
Omnidawn , "On Arthur Sze's Syzygy " by David Koehn
Seismopolite , "The Ecopoetrics of Space in Snyder, Merwin, and Sze"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal Papers consist of materials related to Sze's early life and education, family, travel, and professional activities.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Conferences and events, Travel, Personal and financial records, Teaching materials, Professional files, and Family papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes conference keynotes, drafts and notes, as well as event programs, correspondence, and other papers related to Sze's participation in lectures, readings, workshops, and award ceremonies.

did.unittitle: Academy of American Poets Board of Chancellors Annual Meeting
Academy of American Poets Board of Chancellors Annual Meeting
Academy of American Poets Board of Chancellors Annual Meeting
Academy of American Poets Board of Chancellors Annual Meeting
Associated Colleges of the South Chinese Studies Conference keynote address
Bucknell University Seminar for Younger Poets
Central New Mexico Community College authors event
Institute of American Indian Arts conference
Institute of American Indian Arts Writers Festival
International Haiku Poetry Conference and Festival
Mountains and Plains Independent Booksellers Association Regional Book Award in Poetry
Museum of Contemporary Art San Diego
San Antonio Inter-American Bookfair and Literary Festival
San Jose Center for Poetry and Literature
Santa Fe University of Art and Design
University of Nebraska at Kearney reading

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains travel correspondence, event programs and ephemera, notes, and photographs. Intermixed in the subseries are Sze's family travel files.

did.unittitle: Rainbow Bridge International Poets Gathering at Slender West Lake, China
Yellow Mountain Poetry Festival, United Kingdom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes Sze's appointment books, awards, grants, publishing royalties, and resumes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Sze's teaching files from the Institute of American Indian Art, where he taught creative writing for over 20 years. Included are unpublished anthologies of students' poetry, prose, and art, as well as files from Sze's fellowship as a Visiting Artist at Mary Baldwin College.

did.unittitle: Photos with Rikki DuCornet, Louise Erdrich and Sherman Alexie
Poetry writing workshop I, II, III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials related to Sze's involvement in professional organizations and poetry projects, as well as materials from his tenure as the first Poet Laureate of Santa Fe from 2006 to 2008.

did.unittitle: Academy of American Poets Dear Poet letters
Academy of American Poets Poem-A-Day guest editor
Poetry trading card by Fact-Simile [Travis MacDonald]
Santa Fe Poet Laureate news clippings

title.part: Taos Journal of International Poetry and Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials pertaining to Sze's early life, including high school and college diplomas, childhood and family photos, and a print copy of a book of paintings by his mother Agnes Sze.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Stratford School grade 5 class
Photograph on the Queen Elizabeth from New York to Cherbourg, France
Photograph of Sarah's bat mitzvah, Temple Beth Shalom, Santa Fe
Cumhuriyet [Turkish newspaper featuring Carol Moldaw's translated poems]

title.part: Agnes Sze: A Collection of Paintings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal and professional photographs, including portraits of Sze, public event appearances, and family photos. There are four groups of color slides and negatives of Sze's family trips to Alaska, China, Hawaii, and Japan, as well as two sets of commercial slide shows bought in China and Japan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Photographs, and Color slides and negatives. Arranged by format.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Photographs can also be found in Series I. Correspondence and Series III. Personal Papers.

did.unittitle: Jacona house by Daryl A. Black
Radius Books party with Susan York
China, Yonghe Gong [Lama Temple] slide show by Beijing Slide Studios
Negatives of Alaska, Hawaii and Japan

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer media, and Audiovisual media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes Sze's electronic writing files, emails, digital photos, and audio and video recordings on flash drives, computer disks, and optical media. It also contains materials separated from Series I. Correspondence and Series III. Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains analog audio and video recordings of Sze's poetry readings, lectures, and other public appearances.

did.unittitle: Center for Contemporary Arts, Santa Fe reading
